verified_structural.title: Citrus canker management — copper sprays (3-week cycle) + windbreaks + sanitation
verified_structural.situation: Once citrus canker is established in a grove, eradication is generally impossible; management aims to suppress lesions to maintain marketable fruit. Copper builds up in soil over years; alternate with non-copper management to slow accumulation

verified_structural.situation: Two routes spread Ophiostoma novo-ulmi: (1) elm bark beetles, primarily Scolytus multistriatus (smaller European elm bark beetle) and Hylurgopinus rufipes (native American), which breed in dead/dying elm wood, emerge carrying fungal spores, and feed on healthy elm twig crotches — inoculating the tree. verified_structural.title: Poison ivy / oak / sumac — wash with surfactant within minutes; urushiol persists
verified_structural.situation: Toxicodendron species (poison ivy, oak, sumac) coat their leaves and stems with urushiol — an oily mixture of 3-n-pentadec(en)yl-catechols. verified_structural.title: Seebeck effect — heat-driven electricity without moving parts
verified_structural.situation: A temperature gradient across two dissimilar conductors (or doped semiconductors) generates a small DC voltage. Stack many such junctions in series and you have a thermoelectric generator (TEG): a solid-state heat-to-electricity converter with no moving parts, no fuel, just a hot side and a cold side.

trail.detail: Seebeck coefficient measured for every conductor and semiconductor; published in standard references.
TEG efficiency 5-8% at consumer scale, up to ~15% in lab. RTGs on Voyager / Curiosity / Perseverance use plutonium-238 + TEG modules for decade-scale power.
Subject to Carnot limit. The 5-8% practical efficiency reflects irreversibilities in finite-time conduction, not a violation of conservation.

trail.detail: Iron-deficiency anemia treatment (Hb normalization in 6-8 weeks at 60-200 mg elemental Fe/day)
No physiologic excretion of iron; uptake regulated at gut; surplus stored as ferritin → free iron → ROS damage

verified_structural.title: Hand-washing with chlorinated lime — Semmelweis (1847)
verified_structural.situation: Hand-washing in chlorinated lime (Ca(OCl)₂) solution between patients reduced puerperal fever mortality in Vienna General Hospital's First Obstetric Clinic from 18.3% (1846) to 2.2% (1848). Modern equivalent: soap + 20 seconds, OR alcohol-based hand rub (60–80% ethanol or isopropanol).

trail.detail: Mortality drop 18.3% → 2.2% over a single year is a >5-sigma effect for n ≈ 4000 births/year
Hand-borne Streptococcus pyogenes was the puerperal fever pathogen; transmission interrupted by handwash

verified_structural.title: Root cellar — soil thermal mass + humidity for winter storage
verified_structural.situation: An underground or earth-bermed chamber at 0.5–2 m depth holds 32–40°F (0–4°C) and 85–95% relative humidity year-round via soil thermal mass and ground-water-table coupling. Root vegetables (potatoes, carrots, beets, parsnips, cabbage) keep 3–6 months with no electricity.

trail.detail: Soil temperature damping: amplitude halves every ~1 m depth; phase lags ~30 days per m
Respiration of stored roots slows ~half per 10°C drop (Q₁₀)

verified_structural.title: Daniell cell — zinc / copper-sulfate wet battery, 1.1V steady
verified_structural.situation: A Daniell cell built from a zinc rod in ZnSO₄ electrolyte separated by a porous barrier from a copper rod in CuSO₄ electrolyte produces a steady terminal voltage of 1.10 V (open-circuit). The cell sustains useful current for telegraphy and clock-driving for months without recharging — the historical workhorse of the 19

trail.detail: Standard electrode potentials sum to 1.10 V; cell shows 1.08–1.10 V open circuit in practice
Internal resistance ~1Ω in classroom-size cell; useful for low-current sustained loads (mA range)

verified_structural.title: Copper electroplating — CuSO₄ + H₂SO₄ + DC current
verified_structural.situation: Electroplating copper onto a conductive substrate uses a copper anode + the object (cathode) in an electrolyte of ~200 g/L CuSO₄ + 50 g/L H₂SO₄ at 1–3 V DC. Faraday's law fixes deposition rate: 1 ampere-hour deposits 1.186 g of copper. Quality depends on current density (~0.02–0.05 A/cm²) and electrolyte cleanliness.

trail.detail: Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Cu (cathode); Cu → Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ (anode). Anode dissolves to replenish; bath stable.
Voltage 1–3 V; current density 0.02–0.05 A/cm² for bright deposit

verified_structural.title: Charcoal retort kiln — pyrolysis at 400–700°C
verified_structural.situation: Heating wood in an oxygen-limited chamber (retort) to 400–700°C drives off volatile compounds (water, tars, methanol, acetic acid, wood gas) leaving behind carbon-rich charcoal (~75–85% carbon by weight). Yield: ~25–35% of dry wood mass becomes charcoal. The released wood gas can be captured and burned to heat the kiln

trail.detail: Pyrolysis temperatures: 200–280°C drying + initial decomp; 280–500°C tar and gas release; 500–700°C completion to high-carbon char
Wood gas energy content ~5 MJ/Nm³; can be burned to drive the kiln

verified_structural.title: Lime mortar — CaO/Ca(OH)₂/CaCO₃ reversible carbon cycle
verified_structural.situation: Three reactions: (1) calcination at 900°C: CaCO₃ → CaO + CO₂; (2) slaking with water: CaO + H₂O → Ca(OH)₂; (3) carbonation in air over months to years: Ca(OH)₂ + CO₂ → CaCO₃ + H₂O. All atoms balance; the cement reabsorbs the CO₂ released in step 1 over the life of the building. Compressive strength 0.5–2 MPa (lower tha

trail.detail: CaCO₃ → CaO + CO₂ (calcination); CaO + H₂O → Ca(OH)₂ (slaking); Ca(OH)₂ + CO₂ → CaCO₃ + H₂O (carbonation). All atoms balance.
Hydraulic lime sets faster (weeks) than air lime (months/years); strength 0.5–2 MPa

verified_structural.title: Concrete 1:2:3 — cement : sand : gravel by volume, ~3000 psi
verified_structural.situation: A concrete mix of 1 part Portland cement, 2 parts clean sharp sand, 3 parts coarse aggregate (3/4" gravel) by volume, combined with water to a w/c ratio of 0.45–0.55, cures to a compressive strength of approximately 20 MPa (3000 psi) at 28 days. Suitable for foundations, slabs, footings, and reinforced beams in light r

trail.detail: Cement hydration produces calcium-silicate-hydrate gel; full strength reached at 28 days (Portland cement industry standard)
1:2:3 mix achieves ~20 MPa at 28 days standard cure (ACI 318 reference)

verified_structural.title: Milk paint — casein + lime + pigment, no VOCs, 200+ years of use
verified_structural.situation: Mixing skim milk (or quark/curds) with hydrated lime (Ca(OH)₂) saponifies the milk protein casein into calcium caseinate — a water-resistant binder. With earth pigments (ochre, umber, iron oxides), this produces a durable matte finish that has protected American colonial furniture and Shaker buildings for centuries.

trail.detail: Casein + Ca(OH)₂ → calcium caseinate (water-resistant film-former); pigments suspend as inert particles in the binder
Vapor-permeable finish; flexes with wood substrate; ~15–25 year life on protected surfaces

verified_structural.title: Concrete masonry units (CMU) — home-made hollow blocks, 1:3:5 mix
verified_structural.situation: Standard concrete blocks 8×8×16 inches (~190×190×390 mm) made in a manual block-press from a low-slump 1:3:5 cement:sand:gravel mix achieve compressive strength of ~7 MPa after 28 days — adequate for non-load-bearing walls and adequately reinforced load-bearing walls in residential construction. One person + one press

trail.detail: Same Portland-cement hydration as standard concrete; lower w/c gives strength + immediate green strength
ASTM C90: medium-weight CMU compressive strength minimum 13.1 MPa NET area for load-bearing; ~7 MPa hand-made blocks suit non-load-bearing or reinforced applications

verified_structural.title: Chlorination dosing — free chlorine residual ≥0.5 mg/L for safe water
verified_structural.situation: Adding sodium hypochlorite (NaClO) to water at 2 mg/L active chlorine for 30 minutes contact time, holding a free chlorine residual of ≥0.5 mg/L, inactivates >99.99% of bacteria, viruses, and Giardia cysts. NaClO + H₂O ⇌ HClO + NaOH; HClO is the active germicide.

trail.detail: NaClO hydrolysis to HClO is pH-dependent; at pH 7, ~75% is HClO
WHO drinking-water guideline: ≥0.5 mg/L free chlorine residual after 30 min contact

verified_structural.title: Oral rehydration solution (ORS) — WHO formula, ~$0.10/dose
verified_structural.situation: 1 liter of safe water + 2.6 g NaCl + 1.5 g KCl + 2.9 g trisodium citrate + 13.5 g glucose = WHO low-osmolarity ORS (245 mOsm/L). Reduces cholera and acute diarrhea mortality from ~50% to <1%. Glucose drives sodium absorption via SGLT1 even when the gut is otherwise leaking water.

trail.detail: Osmolarity ≈ (Na⁺ + K⁺ + Cl⁻ + citrate + glucose) ≈ 245 mOsm/L
Lancet 1978: ORS reduces case-fatality of severe dehydrating diarrhea from 30–50% to <1%

verified_structural.title: Iodine tincture antiseptic — broad-spectrum since 1839
verified_structural.situation: 2% iodine + 2.4% sodium iodide in 47% ethanol (USP Tincture of Iodine) kills bacteria, viruses, fungi, and protozoa on contact. Applied to skin pre-procedure, it reduces surgical-site infection. 5 drops of 2% iodine per liter of water purifies it in 30 minutes (emergency dose; not for chronic use due to thyroid load).

trail.detail: I₂ in ethanol stays dissolved; KI or NaI shifts equilibrium to I₃⁻ keeping it soluble
Pre-surgical iodine reduces SSI; emergency water purification at 5 drops 2%/L × 30 min

verified_structural.title: Aloe vera gel — cooling + mild anti-inflammatory for minor burns and sunburn
verified_structural.situation: Aloe vera gel (from Aloe barbadensis leaves) applied to minor first-degree and small second-degree burns reduces pain and accelerates re-epithelialization. Active components: acemannan (polysaccharide, anti-inflammatory), glucomannan (mucilage, cooling/occlusive), low concentrations of salicylates. Mechanism: surface c

trail.detail: Acemannan + glucomannan polysaccharides with documented anti-inflammatory cell-culture effects
Cochrane meta-analyses + clinical practice guidelines recognize aloe for minor (1st-degree, small 2nd-degree) burns

verified_structural.title: Peppermint oil (enteric-coated) — menthol smooth-muscle relaxant for IBS
verified_structural.situation: Enteric-coated peppermint oil capsules (180–225 mg) taken 3× daily reduce overall IBS symptoms in 60–70% of patients vs ~40% placebo. Active compound is menthol, which blocks L-type calcium channels in intestinal smooth muscle and provides direct relaxation. Enteric coating is essential — uncoated peppermint oil releas

trail.detail: Menthol acts at L-type Ca channels and on TRPM8 receptors; smooth-muscle relaxation predominates clinically
Meta-analyses + ACG 2021 guidelines: peppermint oil recommended for IBS pain and global symptoms

verified_structural.title: Ginger — 1g/day for nausea (pregnancy, chemo, motion, post-op)
verified_structural.situation: Ginger (Zingiber officinale) root, taken at 1–1.5 grams per day in divided doses, reduces nausea and vomiting in pregnancy (NVP), chemotherapy-induced nausea, motion sickness, and post-operative nausea. Multiple RCTs and meta-analyses converge on efficacy. Active compounds: gingerols (fresh) and shogaols (dried) act on

trail.detail: Gingerols and shogaols antagonize 5-HT3 receptors (same target as ondansetron); also inhibit prostaglandin synthesis
ACOG: ginger first-line for NVP. Cochrane: effective for nausea in pregnancy, chemo, motion sickness, post-op

verified_structural.title: Willow bark — salicin → salicylic acid; the original aspirin, same effects + risks
verified_structural.situation: Willow bark (Salix alba and related species) contains salicin, a glycoside that is metabolized in the body to salicylic acid — the active metabolite that aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid) is acetylated for. 240 mg salicin (~120–240 mg salicylic acid in vivo) provides analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect comparable to ~5

trail.detail: Salicin → saligenin → salicylic acid (hepatic). Acetylsalicylic acid (aspirin) is acetylated SA with shorter onset, less GI irritation
Willow bark 240 mg salicin daily ≈ low-dose aspirin for back pain (Schmid 2001 RCT)

verified_structural.title: Witch hazel — tannins astringent for minor skin, hemorrhoids, insect bites
verified_structural.situation: Witch hazel extract (Hamamelis virginiana) contains 4–10% tannins (gallic acid derivatives) that precipitate skin proteins, causing tissue tightening and reducing minor inflammation, oozing, and itching. Topical application is well-tolerated and broadly safe. Indicated uses: minor skin irritation, insect bites, hemorrh

trail.detail: Hamamelitannin + gallic acid derivatives bind protein side-chains, cross-linking surface tissue
USP and BP monographs; topical astringent and anti-inflammatory; safe for adults and children

verified_structural.title: Activated charcoal — emergency poisoning only; 'detox' uses are MISMATCH
verified_structural.situation: Activated charcoal (highly porous carbon, ~1000 m²/g surface area) adsorbs many drugs and toxins via van der Waals forces. Clinical use is well-established: oral activated charcoal 1 g/kg within 1 hour of acute ingestion of certain poisons (acetaminophen, aspirin, tricyclic antidepressants, many others) reduces systemi

trail.detail: High surface area (~1000 m²/g) adsorbs many small organic molecules in gut lumen
Standard ER intervention for many acute oral overdoses if ingestion within 1 hour; not effective for alcohols, heavy metals, lithium, iron

verified_structural.title: Vitamin D — CONFIRMED for deficiency (25-OH <20 ng/mL); MIXED for healthy populations
verified_structural.situation: Vitamin D3 supplementation at 1000–4000 IU/day raises serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D. CONFIRMED benefits in deficient populations: rickets (children), osteomalacia (adults), reduced fracture risk in elderly with low baseline. MIXED in healthy populations with adequate baseline: VITAL trial (n=25,871, NEJM 2019) was largely

trail.detail: Deficiency causes rickets/osteomalacia; supplementation corrects (Cochrane)
Cholecalciferol → 25-OH-D (liver) → 1,25-(OH)2-D (kidney) → VDR transcription

verified_structural.title: CoQ10 — MIXED; CONFIRMED for heart failure (Q-SYMBIO); statin myalgia evidence mixed; anti-aging claims unsupported
verified_structural.situation: Coenzyme Q10 is an electron carrier in the mitochondrial respiratory chain, essential for ATP production. Endogenously produced; levels decline with age and in some disease states. CONFIRMED: Q-SYMBIO trial (n=420, JACC 2014) showed 43% reduction in major cardiac events at 100 mg × 3 daily in chronic heart failure. MIX

trail.detail: Electron carrier in mitochondrial Complex III; essential for oxidative phosphorylation
Q-SYMBIO: 43% reduction in MACE in chronic heart failure with 300 mg/day

verified_structural.title: Whey protein — CONFIRMED for convenience and protein adequacy; MIXED for hypertrophy beyond dietary protein
verified_structural.situation: Whey protein is a high-bioavailability complete protein (~24 g protein per scoop) extracted from milk. CONFIRMED utility: meeting daily protein targets (~1.6-2.2 g/kg for resistance trainees), post-workout convenience, fast leucine absorption. MIXED claim: that whey protein per se produces hypertrophy beyond simply mee

trail.detail: Complete protein with high BCAA content; leucine triggers mTOR pathway for muscle synthesis
Whey protein concentrate ~80% protein; isolate ~90%; hydrolysate pre-digested for faster absorption

verified_structural.title: Fish oil (EPA/DHA) — MIXED for general cardiovascular; CONFIRMED for hypertriglyceridemia at prescription dose
verified_structural.situation: Omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (EPA + DHA) are essential structural components of cell membranes and precursors of anti-inflammatory eicosanoids. Cardiovascular outcomes from supplementation have been revised downward repeatedly: VITAL (n=25,871, 2019), ASCEND (n=15,480, 2018), and STRENGTH (n=13,078, 2020) all nu

trail.detail: EPA + DHA: essential, structural, eicosanoid precursors
Multiple large RCTs converge on null for primary prevention; subgroups have signal

verified_structural.title: Rocket stove — L-feed insulated combustion chamber (Winiarski 1980s)
verified_structural.situation: An L-shaped feed (horizontal stick chamber + vertical insulated combustion chimney) with a tight pot skirt achieves near-complete wood combustion. Thermal efficiency to pot reaches 30–45%, compared to 5–15% for an open three-stone fire. Wood consumption per cooking task drops 4–7x.

trail.detail: Open fire ~5–15% efficient to pot; rocket stove 30–45% (Aprovecho lab measurements)
Combustion 2C + O₂ → 2CO + heat; CO + ½O₂ → CO₂ + more heat; both go to completion above 700°C

verified_structural.title: Rocket mass heater — 70–90% efficient wood heat into thermal-mass bench
verified_structural.situation: Rocket-stove combustion (insulated J-tube, internal temperatures 800–1000°C, near-complete burn) routed through a horizontal duct embedded in a cob or masonry bench. The thermal mass absorbs 70–90% of the heat that would escape an ordinary stovepipe, releasing it slowly over 12–24 hours. Wood consumption per heating se

trail.detail: Exhaust temperatures often <100°C (vs 200–400°C for conventional stoves); difference is heat retained in mass
J-tube combustion temperatures >800°C → complete oxidation of CO and volatiles → near-zero PM2.5 emissions

verified_structural.title: Bicycle generator — 50–100W sustainable human electrical output
verified_structural.situation: A trained adult sustains 60–100W mechanical output for hours; with 80–85% efficient permanent-magnet generator + rectifier + voltage regulator, ~50–80W of usable DC electrical output. Enough to run LED lighting (3 × 5W), a radio (3W), a phone charger (5W), or charge a 12V battery (slowly).

trail.detail: Sustained human cycling output: 75W untrained, 150W trained (FTP)
PM generator efficiency 75–90%; net output 50–80% of mechanical input

verified_structural.title: Crystal radio — AM reception with zero external power
verified_structural.situation: An LC tank circuit (coil + variable capacitor) tunes to an AM broadcast frequency; a galena or germanium-diode point-contact detector demodulates the AM envelope; high-impedance headphones (≥2 kΩ) reproduce audio. Energy comes entirely from the broadcast signal itself; no batteries, no transistors, no IC. A long antenn

trail.detail: Resonant f = 1/(2π√LC); typical AM band 540–1700 kHz; L = 240 µH + C = 365 pF max tunes the band
Galena (PbS) and germanium are natural semiconductors; their I/V asymmetry rectifies the carrier

verified_structural.title: Yagi-Uda antenna — 1926, directional gain from parasitic elements
verified_structural.situation: A driven half-wave dipole + one reflector (5% longer than driven, λ/4 behind) + 1–N directors (each 5% shorter than the previous, λ/4 spacing in front) produces a directional radiation pattern with 6–14 dBi forward gain. The directors are not connected — only inductive coupling reshapes the pattern.

trail.detail: Half-wave dipole λ/2 long; director spacing 0.15–0.25 λ optimum; theoretical max gain ~12 dBi for 6-element
Driven element ~73Ω; matched via balun or gamma match to 50Ω coax

verified_structural.title: Solar water heater — copper coil + glazing + insulated box
verified_structural.situation: A copper pipe coiled inside a black-painted, insulated wooden box with a glass cover absorbs solar energy and transfers it to water flowing through (or stored in) the coil. At 6 kWh/m²/day insolation and ~50% net thermal efficiency, a 2 m² collector heats ~50 L of water from 15°C to 50°C in a sunny day — enough for a h

trail.detail: Energy needed: 50kg × 4.18 kJ/kgK × 35K = 7.3 MJ ≈ 2 kWh
2 m² × 6 kWh/m²/day × 50% efficiency = 6 kWh/day available, far exceeds 2 kWh needed

verified_structural.title: Safety bicycle — Rover (Starley, 1885) + pneumatic tire (Dunlop, 1888)
verified_structural.situation: Diamond-frame bicycle with same-size wheels, chain drive (typical 2:1 gear ratio at the rear wheel), and pneumatic tires lets a person travel 3–5× their walking speed for the same metabolic cost. ~20 km/h sustainable on flat over hours; 5–10 W/kg metabolic load. Multiplies a human's effective working range by 4–5×.

trail.detail: Rolling resistance Crr ≈ 0.005 on pavement; aero drag dominates above 25 km/h
Cycling is the most metabolically efficient human locomotion: ~5 kJ/(km·kg) vs ~15 for walking

verified_structural.title: TTT and TTC both code for phenylalanine — synonymous codons (wobble base)
verified_structural.situation: Both TTT and TTC translate to phenylalanine (F) under the standard genetic code. The genetic code is redundant: 64 codons map to 20 amino acids + stop, so most amino acids have multiple synonymous codons.

trail.detail: TTT → F and TTC → F (synonymous codons, wobble base)
Redundant code: 64 codewords mapping to 21 outputs (20 amino acids + stop), surplus = 43 redundant codons.

verified_structural.title: Morse code — variable-length encoding (Vail/Morse, 1838)
verified_structural.situation: Morse code maps each letter to a unique sequence of dots (1 unit) and dashes (3 units), with intra-character gaps of 1 unit, inter-character gaps of 3 units, and inter-word gaps of 7 units. Letter frequency in English roughly inversely correlates with code length (E=•, T=-, both single-unit). Practical hand-key speeds:

trail.detail: Variable-length encoding; average code length ~10 units/letter in English; SNR floor below voice
Code length anti-correlates with letter frequency (Vail's printer type-case analysis)

verified_structural.title: Roubo workbench — massive top + leg vise + holdfast (1769)
verified_structural.situation: A solid-wood workbench with a 4–6" thick laminated top, sliding leg vise, and holdfast-hole array provides a work-holding surface mass-stable against hand-tool forces. The design (André Roubo, *L'Art du Menuisier*, 1769) is the universal pattern for hand-tool woodworking: top thick enough that a chisel mortise doesn't

trail.detail: 100 kg of bench mass + held-down workpiece reduces planing-stroke vibration by ~70% vs. lightweight bench
Roubo design (Plate 11, L'Art du Menuisier) has been continuously reproduced in PD form since 1769

verified_structural.title: Sand casting — green sand + wooden pattern → aluminum or lead castings
verified_structural.situation: Pattern-imprinted molding sand (silica sand + 5–10% bentonite clay + 3–5% water) in a two-part flask provides a cavity into which molten metal (aluminum at 660°C, lead at 327°C, bronze at 950°C) is poured. After solidification (seconds to minutes), the sand is broken away to reveal the cast object. The same basic proce

trail.detail: Aluminum melts 660°C; pour at 720–760°C for fluidity; cools/solidifies in green sand without sticking
Bentonite-bonded green sand (90% silica + 8% bentonite + 4% water) is the standard formulation, unchanged in 100+ years

verified_structural.title: Coal forge with hand bellows — wrought iron and steel work from 800–1300°C
verified_structural.situation: A clay-lined firepot fed by hand-pumped bellows or hand-cranked blower burns bituminous coal or coke at 1100–1400°C — hot enough to make steel red, orange, and yellow, soft enough to hammer-forge into tools, hardware, and structural shapes. The basic configuration has powered village blacksmithing for 3000 years.

trail.detail: Forge temperatures 1100–1400°C achievable with hand bellows; steel forge-welds at ~1300°C (white-hot)
Hot working below recrystallization temperature (~723°C for steel) is warm-working; above is hot-working — both reshape without machining

verified_structural.title: Glass cutting — score and break, perfect edge in 2 seconds
verified_structural.situation: A carbide or diamond scoring wheel run firmly across glass in a single pass creates a microcrack that propagates the full depth on controlled bending pressure, producing a clean fracture along the score line. The mechanism is brittle-crack propagation guided by an engineered stress concentrator — works on any silicate

trail.detail: Brittle fracture mechanics: K_IC × stress concentration exceeds threshold at score apex; crack propagates at sound speed in glass (~3000 m/s)
Score must be a single firm pass — multiple passes weaken the edge by competing cracks

verified_structural.title: Tool sharpening — bevel angle by task, 15° to 35°
verified_structural.situation: Cutting tool edge geometry trades sharpness against durability: 15° (razor, paring knife) is supremely sharp but fragile; 25–30° (chef's knife, wood chisel, plane iron) is the universal balance; 30–35° (axe, hatchet, machete) trades some keenness for impact resistance. Edge longevity scales roughly as bevel angle squar

trail.detail: Wedge force: F_split = F_push × cot(θ/2). For θ=30°: F_split = 3.7 × F_push
Edge retention is a function of bevel angle, steel hardness (Rockwell C), and impact mode

verified_structural.title: Multivitamin (general daily) — modest cancer signal, null cardiovascular, healthy diet still primary
verified_structural.situation: A daily multivitamin/multimineral provides 50–200% RDA of major nutrients in one pill. Best evidence base: Physicians' Health Study II (n=14,641, 11-year follow-up, JAMA 2012) showed modest reduction in total cancer incidence (~8% RR reduction, 0.92, p=0.04) but no benefit for cardiovascular events or all-cause mortali

trail.detail: Provides RDA of major micronutrients in one pill (chemistry is reliable)
Effect sizes detected only in well-powered trials; individual user cannot detect benefit

verified_structural.title: Parabolic solar cooker — geometric focal-point concentration
verified_structural.situation: A parabolic reflector with focal length f, paraboloid equation y = x²/(4f), reflects parallel sun rays to a single focal point. At 1 m² aperture and 800 W/m² insolation, focal-point irradiance reaches 80+ kW/m² (theoretical) and 5–50 kW/m² practical; cooking temperatures exceed 200°C; water boils in 5–15 minutes.

trail.detail: Paraboloid focuses parallel rays at the focal point (defining property)
1 m² × 800 W/m² × 50% optical efficiency ÷ small focal area → cooking temperatures
